qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking (CSWSH) due to missing origin validation in WebSocket handler
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding identifies a missing origin check in the RPC WebSocket handler (rpc/jsonrpc/server/ws_handler.go). The advisory describes an inconsistency between commit signature verification and block time derivation in the consensus engine's BFT Time implementation. These are different components (RPC layer vs consensus layer), different root causes (missing origin validation vs time derivation inconsistency), and different vulnerability classes (CSWSH vs consensus time manipulation).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-c32p-wcqj-j677) describes a consensus-level vulnerability in CometBFT's BFT Time implementation: an inconsistency between how commit signatures are verified and how block time is derived, allowing a faulty process to arbitrarily increase the Time value. The only qualifying finding (cswsh-websocket-origin-bypass) identifies a missing origin check in the RPC WebSocket handler at rpc/jsonrpc/server/ws_handler.go:49-59. This is a different component (RPC vs consensus), different root cause (missing CheckOrigin validation vs BFT Time derivation inconsistency), and different vulnerability class (Cross-Site WebSocket Hijacking vs consensus time manipulation, CWE-703). No finding matches the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory root cause: inconsistency between commit signature verification and block time derivation in consensus engine (CWE-703)
Finding root cause: CheckOrigin function unconditionally returns true in rpc/jsonrpc/server/ws_handler.go:49-59
Advisory affected component: consensus-level BFT Time implementation
Finding affected component: RPC WebSocket handler in rpc/jsonrpc/server/ws_handler.go
Advisory references patch commit bf8274fcdbcab2bc652660ae627196a90a6efb97 which modifies consensus time logic
Finding code locations are entirely in the RPC layer, not the consensus engine
